steps.title: Use the definition after the dash
steps.content: The sentence explains the blank immediately after the dash: the researchers want to see whether differences in age and sex are associated with considerable variations in venom potency. The blank must therefore mean large or meaningful.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong meanings
steps.content: Choice A, “a disconcerting,” means troubling, not necessarily large. Choice B, “an acceptable,” does not match considerable variation. Choice C, “an imperceptible,” means too slight to notice, the opposite of considerable. The word meaning large or important fits. The answer is D.
